stemHtml: Which are some of the most critical types of perceptual illusions?

options.a: Geometric illusion (e.g. depth perception), somatosensory illusion (e.g. autokinesis), and vestibular illusion (e.g. feeling dizzy).
options.b: Visual illusion (e.g. blind spots), kinaesthetic illusion (e.g. the leans), and auditory illusion (e.g. missed radio call).
options.c: Haptic illusion (e.g. colour-blind), vestibular illusion (e.g. graveyard spiral), and sensory illusion (e.g. spatial disorientation).
options.d: Coriolis illusion (e.g. constant turning), neurological illusion (e.g. after-images), and auditory illusion (e.g. hearing voices).
